toolsets.name: ToolsetRegistry.AgentSkillToolset
toolsets.description: Provides tools for listing, reading, and creating/updating skills.

tools.name: ToolsetRegistry.AgentSkillToolset.UpdateSkill
tools.description:
Updates an existing AgentSkill.
This should ONLY be called after getting explicit direction or permission from the user.

skillPath.description: The full path to the skill to modify i.e. /Game/Skills/MySkill.MySkill_C.

description.description: The brief description of the skill.

details.description: Detailed information about how to use the skill.

tools.name: ToolsetRegistry.AgentSkillToolset.ListSkills
tools.description: Gets a summary of all AgentSkills in the project.

tools.name: ToolsetRegistry.AgentSkillToolset.GetSkills
tools.description: Returns detailed information about a specific set of AgentSkills.

skillPaths.description: A list of paths to the AgentSkills to retrieve.

tools.name: ToolsetRegistry.AgentSkillToolset.CreateSkill
tools.description:
Creates a new AgentSkill.
This should ONLY be called after getting explicit direction or permission from the user.

folderPath.description: The folder in which to create the skill. i.e. /Game/Skills/.

assetName.description: The name of the skill to create i.e. MySkill.

description.description: The brief description of the skill.

details.description: Detailed information about how to use the skill.

toolsets.name: AutomationTestToolset.AutomationTestToolset
toolsets.description:
Automation test discovery and execution toolset.

Wraps the IAutomationControllerManager API (the same backend the Session
Frontend uses) to let MCP clients list available tests, run them, and
retrieve results.

Typical workflow:
  1. DiscoverTests() once per session to initialize workers and load the test list.
  2. ListTests() to find tests by name or tag.
  3. RunTests() with the desired test names.
  4. GetTestStatus() / GetTestResults() to monitor and retrieve results.
  5. StopTests() to abort if needed.

tools.name: AutomationTestToolset.AutomationTestToolset.StopTests
tools.description:
Stop all currently running tests. Requires DiscoverTests() to have completed.
If a RunTests async result is pending, it will be completed with an error.

tools.name: AutomationTestToolset.AutomationTestToolset.RunTestsByFilter
tools.description:
Run automation tests selected by a filter expression. Requires DiscoverTests()
to have completed. Much faster than RunTests when targeting a large batch
because the engine narrows the report tree in a single pass instead of
running a per-leaf membership check against the requested name list.

Filter syntax (multiple expressions joined by '+'):
 "StartsWith:System.Engine"  prefix match against the full test path
 "^Foo"                       prefix anchor (equivalent to StartsWith:)
 "Bar$"                       suffix anchor
 "Substring"                  bare token matches anywhere in the path
 "Group:Smoke"                expand a named group from
                              AutomationControllerSettings ini Groups

Returns an async result that completes with the same JSON summary as RunTests.

tools.name: AutomationTestToolset.AutomationTestToolset.RunTests
tools.description:
Run a set of automation tests by name. Requires DiscoverTests() to have completed.
Starts executing the specified tests and returns an async result that
completes with a JSON summary when all tests finish.

testNames.description: Array of full test paths as returned by ListTests.

tools.name: AutomationTestToolset.AutomationTestToolset.ListTests
tools.description:
List available automation tests. Requires DiscoverTests() to have completed.
Returns a JSON object: {"tests": ["path1", ...], "total": N, "returned": N}.

nameFilter.description: Optional substring filter applied to the test's full path.

tagFilter.description: Optional substring filter applied to the test's tags.

limit.description: Maximum number of tests to return (0 = unlimited, default 200).

tools.name: AutomationTestToolset.AutomationTestToolset.GetTestStatus
tools.description:
Get a lightweight status snapshot of the automation controller.
Requires DiscoverTests() to have completed.
Returns a JSON object with the controller state, enabled test count,
and completion/pass/fail counts.

tools.name: AutomationTestToolset.AutomationTestToolset.GetTestResults
tools.description:
Get detailed results for the current or most recent test run.
Requires DiscoverTests() to have completed.
Returns a JSON object with per-test state, duration, errors, and warnings.

tools.name: AutomationTestToolset.AutomationTestToolset.DiscoverTests
tools.description:
Initialize automation worker discovery and load the test list.
Must be called once before ListTests or RunTests. Takes several seconds
as it discovers the local automation worker and enumerates all registered
tests. Returns an async result that completes with a JSON status object
when tests are available, or an error if discovery fails.

bForceRediscover.description: When true, bypass the cached report tree and re-poll workers. Used after reloading Python test modules mid-session.

toolsets.name: ConfigSettingsToolset.ConfigSettingsToolset
toolsets.description: Tools for listing, inspecting, and editing Config Settings sections

tools.name: ConfigSettingsToolset.ConfigSettingsToolset.SetSectionProperties
tools.description:
Sets one or more properties on a settings section from a JSON object and saves.
PropertiesJson must be a JSON object mapping property names to new values,
in the same format returned by GetSectionPropertyValues.
Raises an error if the section does not exist, cannot be edited, has no settings
object, the default config file is not writable, or any property cannot be set.

propertiesJson.description: JSON object with property name to new value pairs.

tools.name: ConfigSettingsToolset.ConfigSettingsToolset.SaveSection
tools.description:
Saves the settings in a section.
Raises an error if the section does not exist or saving is not supported.

tools.name: ConfigSettingsToolset.ConfigSettingsToolset.ResetSectionToDefaults
tools.description:
Resets the settings in a section to their default values.
Raises an error if the section does not exist or reset is not supported.

tools.name: ConfigSettingsToolset.ConfigSettingsToolset.ListSections
tools.description:
Lists the names of all sections within a settings category, sorted alphabetically.
Raises an error if the container or category does not exist.

tools.name: ConfigSettingsToolset.ConfigSettingsToolset.ListContainers
tools.description:
Lists the names of all known settings containers, sorted alphabetically.
Common containers are "Editor" and "Project".

tools.name: ConfigSettingsToolset.ConfigSettingsToolset.ListCategories
tools.description:
Lists the names of all categories within a settings container, sorted alphabetically.
Raises an error if the container does not exist.

tools.name: ConfigSettingsToolset.ConfigSettingsToolset.GetSectionSchema
tools.description:
Returns a JSON Schema describing the user-visible properties of a settings section.
The schema maps each property name to its type, description, and constraints.
Raises an error if the section does not exist or has no backing settings object
(e.g. uses a custom widget instead).

tools.name: ConfigSettingsToolset.ConfigSettingsToolset.GetSectionPropertyValues
tools.description:
Returns the current values of the specified properties as a JSON object.
Raises an error if the section does not exist, has no settings object, or any
requested property cannot be read.

propertyNames.description: The names of the properties to read.

toolsets.name: DataRegistryToolset.DataRegistryTools
toolsets.description: Provides tools for querying and inspecting Data Registries.

tools.name: DataRegistryToolset.DataRegistryTools.ListRuntimeSources
tools.description:
Returns the runtime sources for a Data Registry.
This is the expanded list including transient child sources generated from meta sources.
Will equal ListDataSources when the registry has no meta sources.

tools.name: DataRegistryToolset.DataRegistryTools.ListRegistries
tools.description: Returns the names of all registered Data Registries.

structFilter.description: If non-null, only registries whose item struct inherits from this struct are returned.

tools.name: DataRegistryToolset.DataRegistryTools.ListItems
tools.description: Returns all item names in a Data Registry.

tools.name: DataRegistryToolset.DataRegistryTools.ListDataSources
tools.description:
Returns the editor-defined sources configured on a Data Registry.
These are the sources as authored on the registry asset, before any runtime expansion
of meta sources.

tools.name: DataRegistryToolset.DataRegistryTools.GetSchema
tools.description: Returns the item struct schema as JSON.

tools.name: DataRegistryToolset.DataRegistryTools.GetRegistryInfo
tools.description: Returns detailed information about a specific registry.

tools.name: DataRegistryToolset.DataRegistryTools.GetItems
tools.description:
Returns cached item data.
Items must be loaded in the registry cache to be returned.

toolsets.name: GameFeaturesToolset.GameFeaturesToolset
toolsets.description: Provides tools for listing, activating, and deactivating Game Feature Plugins.

tools.name: GameFeaturesToolset.GameFeaturesToolset.RequestDeactivateGameFeature
tools.description:
Requests deactivation of a Game Feature Plugin.
Returns true if the deactivation request was submitted successfully. The actual deactivation
happens asynchronously -- poll GetGameFeatureState() to confirm completion.
Raises an error if the subsystem is unavailable or the plugin is not found.

tools.name: GameFeaturesToolset.GameFeaturesToolset.RequestActivateGameFeature
tools.description:
Requests activation of a Game Feature Plugin.
Returns true if the activation request was submitted successfully. The actual activation
happens asynchronously -- poll GetGameFeatureState() or IsGameFeatureActive()
to confirm completion. Raises an error if the subsystem is unavailable
or the plugin is not found.

tools.name: GameFeaturesToolset.GameFeaturesToolset.ListEnabledGameFeaturePlugins
tools.description:
Lists all enabled Game Feature Plugins  sorted by name. Enabled plugins are the only plugins
known by the Game Features system beyond identifying if a plugin is a Game Feature Plugin.
Use the Plugins toolset to do general plugin enable/disable tasks.

tools.name: GameFeaturesToolset.GameFeaturesToolset.ListDiscoveredGameFeaturePlugins
tools.description:
Lists all discovered Game Feature Plugins sorted by name. This includes enabled and disabled
plugins. Only enabled plugins are known by the Game Features system beyond identifying if a
plugin is a Game Feature Plugin.
Use the Plugins toolset to do general plugin enable/disable tasks.

tools.name: GameFeaturesToolset.GameFeaturesToolset.IsGameFeaturePlugin
tools.description:
Return whether or not a plugin is a Game Feature Plugin. Will error if no plugin of this
name can be found by the Plugin Manager.

tools.name: GameFeaturesToolset.GameFeaturesToolset.IsGameFeatureActive
tools.description:
Checks whether a Game Feature Plugin is active. Raises an error if the subsystem is unavailable
or the plugin is not found.
Use GetGameFeatureState if you need the current state when the plugin is not active.

pluginName.description: Name of the Game Feature Plugin.

tools.name: GameFeaturesToolset.GameFeaturesToolset.GetGameFeatureState
tools.description: Gets the current state of a Game Feature Plugin.

pluginName.description: Name of the Game Feature Plugin.

toolsets.name: DataflowAgent.DataflowAgentToolset
toolsets.description: Dataflow Agent Toolset - exposes Dataflow graph editing operations as AI agent tools.

tools.name: DataflowAgent.DataflowAgentToolset.UpdateNode
tools.description: Updates an existing node's editable properties via JSON.

node.description: The EdNode to modify

tools.name: DataflowAgent.DataflowAgentToolset.SetVariable
tools.description:
Sets the value of an existing variable using its serialized string representation.
The format depends on the variable's type (e.g., "3.14" for float, "true" for bool,
"42" for int, "MyName" for FName).

graph.description: The Dataflow asset to modify

name.description: Name of the variable to set

tools.name: DataflowAgent.DataflowAgentToolset.RepositionNode
tools.description: Moves a node to a new position in the graph editor.

node.description: The EdNode to reposition

tools.name: DataflowAgent.DataflowAgentToolset.RemoveVariable
tools.description: Removes a variable from the Dataflow graph.

graph.description: The Dataflow asset to modify

name.description: Name of the variable to remove

tools.name: DataflowAgent.DataflowAgentToolset.RemoveNode
tools.description: Removes a node and all its connections from the Dataflow graph.

graph.description: The Dataflow asset containing the node

node.description: The EdNode to remove

tools.name: DataflowAgent.DataflowAgentToolset.RemoveCommentBox
tools.description: Removes a comment box node from the graph.

graph.description: The Dataflow asset containing the comment

commentId.description: The node GUID string returned by AddCommentBox

tools.name: DataflowAgent.DataflowAgentToolset.ListVariables
tools.description:
Returns all variables defined on the Dataflow graph as a JSON array.
Each entry contains "name", "type", and "value" fields.

graph.description: The Dataflow asset to inspect

tools.name: DataflowAgent.DataflowAgentToolset.ListNodeTypes
tools.description: Returns a JSON list of all registered Dataflow node types.

bCommonOnly.description: When true, only return non-deprecated non-experimental nodes. Default is true.

tools.name: DataflowAgent.DataflowAgentToolset.ListDataflowTemplatesForAssetClass
tools.description:
Returns a JSON list of Dataflow templates registered for the given asset class.
Templates registered for parent classes are included (class hierarchy walk).

className.description: Asset class name with or without the "U"/"A" prefix

bIncludeBlank.description: When true, include a "Blank" option (empty graph) at the start

tools.name: DataflowAgent.DataflowAgentToolset.ListDataflowCompatibleAssetTypes
tools.description:
Returns a JSON list of every UClass that can host an embedded Dataflow graph
(i.e. implements IDataflowInstanceInterface). Each entry has "className",
"displayName", and "modulePath" fields.

Use the "className" value as input to CreateDataflowCompatibleAsset or
ListDataflowTemplatesForAssetClass.

tools.name: DataflowAgent.DataflowAgentToolset.GetNodeTypeSchema
tools.description:
Returns the schema for a Dataflow node type including its input/output pins
and editable UPROPERTY parameters.

tools.name: DataflowAgent.DataflowAgentToolset.GetNodeInfo
tools.description: Returns information about a node as a JSON object (name, type, position, pins).

node.description: The EdNode to query

tools.name: DataflowAgent.DataflowAgentToolset.GetGraphStructure
tools.description: Returns the complete structure of a Dataflow graph including all nodes and connections.

graph.description: The Dataflow asset to inspect

tools.name: DataflowAgent.DataflowAgentToolset.DisconnectNodePins
tools.description: Removes the connection between two node pins.

fromPin.description: Name of the output pin on the source node

toPin.description: Name of the input pin on the destination node

tools.name: DataflowAgent.DataflowAgentToolset.CreateGraph
tools.description: Creates a new saved Dataflow graph asset.

name.description: Name for the new Dataflow asset (e.g., "DF_Simulation")

path.description: Content folder path where the asset should be created. Defaults to "/Game/Dataflow"

tools.name: DataflowAgent.DataflowAgentToolset.CreateDataflowCompatibleAssetFromTemplate
tools.description:
Creates a new Dataflow-compatible asset and initialises its embedded Dataflow graph
from a registered template in one step.

className.description: Asset class name with or without the "U"/"A" prefix

templateId.description: Template identifier returned by ListDataflowTemplatesForAssetClass. Pass an empty string to create with an empty embedded graph (equivalent to CreateDataflowCompatibleAsset).

tools.name: DataflowAgent.DataflowAgentToolset.CreateDataflowCompatibleAsset
tools.description:
Creates a new Dataflow-compatible asset (e.g. ChaosClothAsset, GeometryCollection,
FleshAsset, GroomAsset) with an empty embedded Dataflow graph.

className.description: Asset class name with or without the "U"/"A" prefix (e.g. "ChaosClothAsset" or "UChaosClothAsset")

path.description: Content folder path where the asset should be created. Defaults to "/Game/Dataflow"

tools.name: DataflowAgent.DataflowAgentToolset.ConnectNodePins
tools.description: Connects an output pin of one node to an input pin of another.

fromPin.description: Name of the output pin on the source node

toPin.description: Name of the input pin on the destination node

tools.name: DataflowAgent.DataflowAgentToolset.AssignDataflowTemplate
tools.description:
Assigns a Dataflow template to an existing Dataflow-compatible asset by duplicating
the template graph and embedding it. Replaces any existing embedded graph.

templateId.description: Template identifier returned by ListDataflowTemplatesForAssetClass. Pass an empty string to assign a fresh empty graph.

tools.name: DataflowAgent.DataflowAgentToolset.AddVariable
tools.description:
Adds a new variable to the Dataflow graph.

Supported type strings:
  Primitives : "Bool", "Int32", "Int64", "Float", "Double", "Name", "String"
  Structs    : UScriptStruct name with or without the "F" prefix
               e.g. "Vector", "FVector", "Transform", "FTransform", "Rotator", "LinearColor"
  Objects    : "Object:<ClassName>" where ClassName is with or without the "U"/"A" prefix
               e.g. "Object:StaticMesh", "Object:USkeletalMesh"

graph.description: The Dataflow asset to modify

name.description: Unique name for the new variable

tools.name: DataflowAgent.DataflowAgentToolset.AddNode
tools.description: Adds a node of the given type to the Dataflow graph.

graph.description: The Dataflow asset to add the node to

nodeName.description: Unique name for the node within this graph

jsonParams.description: Optional JSON object of property overrides (e.g., {"Value": 3.14})

x.description: X position in the graph editor (default 0, typical node width ~200)

y.description: Y position in the graph editor (default 0, typical node height ~100)

tools.name: DataflowAgent.DataflowAgentToolset.AddCommentBox
tools.description: Adds a comment box around the given nodes.

graph.description: The Dataflow asset to add the comment to

nodes.description: List of EdNodes to surround with the comment box

comment.description: Text to display on the comment box

color.description: Background color of the comment box (defaults to White)

toolsets.name: EditorToolset.EditorAppToolset
toolsets.description:
Tools for querying and modifying Unreal Editor state: console variables, asset imaging,
actor and asset selection, viewport camera, content browser navigation, and Play-In-Editor
session control.

tools.name: EditorToolset.EditorAppToolset.WorldPosToScreenCoords
tools.description: Converts a world-space position into normalized screen space based on the editor viewport camera.

position.description: The world space position to convert.

tools.name: EditorToolset.EditorAppToolset.StopPIE
tools.description:
Stops the currently running play session (PIE or Simulate).
Raises an error if no play session is running.

tools.name: EditorToolset.EditorAppToolset.StartPIE
tools.description:
Starts a Play-In-Editor or Simulate-In-Editor session using the current level.
Completes after the engine fires PostPIEStarted (session fully started,
BeginPlay called) and Options.WarmupSeconds have elapsed, giving project-
specific initialization (services, authentication, plugin warmup) time to
settle before the agent inspects state or logs.
Raises an error if a play session is already running.

options.description: Session configuration: PIE vs Simulate, play mode, optional spawn transform override, warmup duration. See FPIESessionOptions.

tools.name: EditorToolset.EditorAppToolset.SetContentBrowserPath
tools.description: Navigates the active content browser to the specified folder path.

path.description: The internal path to navigate to, e.g. '/Game/Meshes'.

tools.name: EditorToolset.EditorAppToolset.SetCameraTransform
tools.description: Sets the position and rotation of the level viewport camera.

transform.description: The transform to apply to the viewport camera.

tools.name: EditorToolset.EditorAppToolset.SelectAssets
tools.description:
Selects the specified assets in the content browser.
Completes once the content browser has applied the selection.

assetPaths.description: The package paths of the assets to select.

tools.name: EditorToolset.EditorAppToolset.SelectActors
tools.description: Selects the specified actors in the current scene.

tools.name: EditorToolset.EditorAppToolset.SearchCVars
tools.description: Finds all console variables that contain a given name.

name.description: The partial or full name to search for.

tools.name: EditorToolset.EditorAppToolset.ScreenCoordsToWorld
tools.description: Finds the world position of the nearest solid object at a given set of normalized view space coords.

coords.description: The normalized screen-space coordinates to trace from.

traceDistance.description: The maximum distance to trace within the scene.

tools.name: EditorToolset.EditorAppToolset.OpenEditorForAsset
tools.description: Opens an asset editor for the specified asset.

assetPath.description: The path to the asset to open, e.g. '/Game/Meshes/SM_Cube'.

tools.name: EditorToolset.EditorAppToolset.IsPIERunning
tools.description: Returns whether a Play In Editor session is currently running.

tools.name: EditorToolset.EditorAppToolset.GetVisibleActors
tools.description: Returns all actors in the current level whose bounds intersect the viewport frustum.

tools.name: EditorToolset.EditorAppToolset.GetSelectedAssets
tools.description: Gets the list of assets selected in the content browser.

tools.name: EditorToolset.EditorAppToolset.GetSelectedActors
tools.description: Gets the currently selected actors in the level editor.

tools.name: EditorToolset.EditorAppToolset.GetOpenAssets
tools.description: Gets the list of assets currently open in asset editors.

tools.name: EditorToolset.EditorAppToolset.GetContentBrowserPath
tools.description: Gets the current path of the active content browser.

tools.name: EditorToolset.EditorAppToolset.GetCameraTransform
tools.description: Returns the position and rotation of the level viewport camera.

tools.name: EditorToolset.EditorAppToolset.FocusOnActors
tools.description:
Repositions the level editor camera to focus on the specified actors.
Cannot be called while PIE is active.

actors.description: The actors to focus the level camera on.

tools.name: EditorToolset.EditorAppToolset.CaptureViewport
tools.description:
Captures the level viewport with optional annotations.

Annotations rendering overlays a projected 3D world-space grid plus
name + position labels on visible actors. The grid is drawn at a configurable
ground-plane Z and projected through the camera, with coordinate numbers at
intersections (shown in meters). Each labeled actor gets a crosshair at its
projected screen position with a leader-line callout placed to avoid overlap. This
gives a vision-capable agent spatial awareness: it can reference grid
coordinates to direct placement and identify scene contents by label.

captureTransform.description: Optional pose to capture from. If unset, uses the viewport's current camera.

annotations.description: Optional annotation overlay configuration. Only use this when you need the information in order to perform spatial actions.

bShowUI.description: If false (default), editor UI overlays such as transform gizmos and selection outlines are hidden in the captured image. Set true to capture exactly what's on screen, gizmos and all.

tools.name: EditorToolset.EditorAppToolset.CaptureEditorImage
tools.description: Captures an image of the entire editor application as the user sees it.

tools.name: EditorToolset.EditorAppToolset.CaptureAssetImage
tools.description:
Renders a thumbnail for the specified asset (e.g. static meshes, skeletal meshes,
skeletons, animations, montages, materials, textures).

assetPath.description: The path to the asset, e.g. '/Game/Meshes/SM_Cube'.

toolsets.name: EditorToolset.LogsToolset
toolsets.description:
Provides tools for reading the Unreal Engine output log and controlling
log category verbosity.

tools.name: EditorToolset.LogsToolset.SetVerbosity
tools.description: Sets the verbosity level for a log category.

verbosity.description: The verbosity level: one of "NoLogging", "Fatal", "Error", "Warning", "Display", "Log", "Verbose", or "VeryVerbose".

tools.name: EditorToolset.LogsToolset.GetVerbosity
tools.description: Returns the current verbosity level for a log category.

tools.name: EditorToolset.LogsToolset.GetLogEntries
tools.description: Returns log entries from the current session's log file.

category.description: If non-empty, only returns entries from this log category (e.g. "LogTemp").

pattern.description: If non-empty, only returns entries whose text matches this regular expression.

maxEntries.description: Maximum number of entries to return, taken from the end of the log. Pass 0 for no limit. Defaults to 1000.

tools.name: EditorToolset.LogsToolset.GetLogCategories
tools.description: Returns a sorted list of registered log categories.

filter.description: If non-empty, only returns categories whose name contains this substring.

toolsets.name: GameplayTagsToolset.GameplayTagsToolset
toolsets.description: Provides tools for reading and managing gameplay tags in the project's GameplayTagsManager.

tools.name: GameplayTagsToolset.GameplayTagsToolset.RenameTag
tools.description:
Renames a gameplay tag, updating all references in the project.
This should ONLY be called after getting explicit direction or permission from the user.

oldTagName.description: The fully-qualified name of the tag to rename.

newTagName.description: The new fully-qualified name for the tag.

tools.name: GameplayTagsToolset.GameplayTagsToolset.RemoveTag
tools.description:
Removes a gameplay tag from the project.
This should ONLY be called after getting explicit direction or permission from the user.

tagName.description: The fully-qualified name of the tag to remove, e.g. "Character.State.Dead".

tools.name: GameplayTagsToolset.GameplayTagsToolset.ListTags
tools.description: Returns gameplay tags registered in the project.

parentTag.description: If non-empty, only tags that are descendants of this tag are returned. For example, passing "Character.State" returns "Character.State.Dead", "Character.State.Stunned", etc. Pass an empty string to return all tags.

tools.name: GameplayTagsToolset.GameplayTagsToolset.GetTagInfo
tools.description: Returns detailed information about a specific gameplay tag.

tagName.description: The fully-qualified name of the tag, e.g. "Character.State.Dead".

tools.name: GameplayTagsToolset.GameplayTagsToolset.FindReferencersByTag
tools.description: Returns assets that reference a gameplay tag.

tagName.description: The fully-qualified gameplay tag to search for, e.g. "Character.State.Dead".

tools.name: GameplayTagsToolset.GameplayTagsToolset.AddTag
tools.description:
Adds a new gameplay tag to the project.
This should ONLY be called after getting explicit direction or permission from the user.

tagName.description: The fully-qualified name of the tag to add, e.g. "Character.State.Dead".

comment.description: An optional developer comment describing the tag's purpose.

tagSource.description: The INI source to add the tag to. Uses the default source if empty.

toolsets.name: GASToolsets.GameplayCueToolset
toolsets.description: Provides tools for inspecting, executing, and managing gameplay cues and their notify assets.

tools.name: GASToolsets.GameplayCueToolset.RemoveCueTag
tools.description:
Removes a gameplay cue tag from the project.
This should ONLY be called after getting explicit direction or permission from the user.

cueTag.description: The fully-qualified cue tag to remove (e.g. "GameplayCue.Character.Death").

tools.name: GASToolsets.GameplayCueToolset.ListCues
tools.description: Returns gameplay cue tags registered in the project.

parentTag.description: If non-empty, only cues descending from this tag are returned. For example, passing "GameplayCue.Character" returns all cues under that namespace. Pass an empty string to return all tags under the "GameplayCue" root.

tools.name: GASToolsets.GameplayCueToolset.GetCueInfo
tools.description: Returns information about a specific gameplay cue, including its notify asset.

cueTag.description: The fully-qualified gameplay cue tag, e.g. "GameplayCue.Character.Death".

tools.name: GASToolsets.GameplayCueToolset.FindCueTagsWithoutNotifies
tools.description:
Returns gameplay cue tags that have no corresponding GameplayCueNotify asset in the project.
Tags without notifies produce no visible effect when triggered at runtime.

tools.name: GASToolsets.GameplayCueToolset.FindCueNotifyAssets
tools.description: Returns all GameplayCueNotify assets found in the project via the asset registry.

parentTag.description: If non-empty, only notifies whose cue tag descends from this tag are returned. Pass an empty string to return all notify assets in the project.

tools.name: GASToolsets.GameplayCueToolset.ExecuteCueOnSelectedActor
tools.description:
Executes a gameplay cue non-replicated on the currently selected actor in the editor.
Useful for previewing cue effects without network replication.
Requires a PIE session or a configured GameplayCueManager to produce visible results.

cueTag.description: The fully-qualified tag of the cue to execute, e.g. "GameplayCue.Character.Death".

normalizedMagnitude.description: A normalized (0.0-1.0) magnitude value passed to the cue.

location.description: World-space location parameter passed to the cue.

normal.description: World-space direction parameter passed to the cue.

tools.name: GASToolsets.GameplayCueToolset.CreateCueNotifyAsset
tools.description:
Creates a new GameplayCueNotify Blueprint asset at the specified content browser location.
This should ONLY be called after getting explicit direction or permission from the user.

cueTag.description: The gameplay cue tag the notify should respond to. The tag must already exist; create it first with AddCueTag if needed.

packagePath.description: The content browser folder for the asset, e.g. "/Game/Effects/Cues".

assetName.description: The file name for the new asset, e.g. "GCN_CharacterDeath".

bIsActor.description: If true, creates a GameplayCueNotify_Actor (spawned actor in the world). If false, creates a GameplayCueNotify_Static (instant effect, no spawned actor).

tools.name: GASToolsets.GameplayCueToolset.AddCueTag
tools.description:
Adds a new gameplay cue tag to the project.
This should ONLY be called after getting explicit direction or permission from the user.

cueTag.description: The fully-qualified tag to add. Must begin with "GameplayCue." (e.g. "GameplayCue.Character.Death").

comment.description: An optional developer comment describing the cue's purpose.

toolsets.name: GASToolsets.AttributeSetToolset
toolsets.description: Provides tools for discovering AttributeSet classes and their gameplay attributes.

tools.name: GASToolsets.AttributeSetToolset.ListAttributes
tools.description: Returns the gameplay attributes defined on a specific AttributeSet class.

toolsets.name: SlateInspectorToolset.SlateInspectorToolset
toolsets.description:
Playwright-style Slate UI automation toolset.

Exposes snapshot, screenshot, and interaction tools for driving the
Unreal Editor UI programmatically.  Registered via UToolsetRegistry
so the ModelContextProtocol plugin picks them up automatically.

A shallow root observer (depth 0) continuously tracks top-level
windows.  Before working with a specific window or panel, call
Observe() on it to get deep widget coverage, then Unobserve() when
done.  Observers walk their subtree every ~100ms, assigning refs to
newly appeared widgets and keeping the ref cache current.

Input simulation uses direct Slate event APIs (ProcessKeyCharEvent,
ProcessMouseButtonDownEvent, etc.) rather than the AutomationDriver,
because AutomationDriver's synchronous API deadlocks when called from
the game thread (which is where MCP tool calls execute).

tools.name: SlateInspectorToolset.SlateInspectorToolset.Windows
tools.description: List, select, or close top-level Slate editor windows.

action.description: "list" returns JSON array, "select" brings to front, "close" destroys.

tools.name: SlateInspectorToolset.SlateInspectorToolset.WaitFor
tools.description:
Check if text is present or absent in the Slate widget tree.
Non-blocking: checks once and returns immediately. Poll to wait.

text.description: Text that must be present (empty = skip).

textGone.description: Text that must be absent (empty = skip).

tools.name: SlateInspectorToolset.SlateInspectorToolset.Unobserve
tools.description: Remove an observer by its identifier.

tools.name: SlateInspectorToolset.SlateInspectorToolset.Type
tools.description:
Type text into a Slate text input widget. Focuses the widget first,
then sends one key event per character.

tools.name: SlateInspectorToolset.SlateInspectorToolset.Snapshot
tools.description:
Capture a Slate UI accessibility snapshot.  Use this to read the current
widget tree and discover refs for action tools (Click, Type, Hover, etc.).
A shallow root observer (depth 0) covers top-level windows automatically.
Before interacting with a specific window or panel, call Observe() on it
to get deep coverage, then Snapshot that subtree to see its contents.
Refs discovered by a previous Snapshot remain usable. You do NOT need to
call Snapshot again before every action.

ref.description: Subtree root ref. Empty = all windows.

bIncludeSourceLocations.description: Include [src=File:Line] tags showing where each widget was created in C++.

tools.name: SlateInspectorToolset.SlateInspectorToolset.SelectOption
tools.description:
Select an option in a Slate combobox by its text label.
Opens the dropdown, finds the matching text, and clicks it.

tools.name: SlateInspectorToolset.SlateInspectorToolset.Screenshot
tools.description:
Screenshot a Slate widget or the active editor window. Prefer this over
SceneTools.take_screenshot for Editor UI; use SceneTools only for 3D viewport.

ref.description: Slate widget ref. Empty = active window.

tools.name: SlateInspectorToolset.SlateInspectorToolset.PressKey
tools.description:
Press and release a keyboard key on the currently focused Slate widget.
Supports modifier prefixes: "Ctrl+C", "Shift+1".

key.description: Key name with optional modifiers, e.g. "Enter", "Ctrl+A", "Shift+Tab".

tools.name: SlateInspectorToolset.SlateInspectorToolset.Observe
tools.description:
Register an observer on a widget subtree so its refs are continuously
kept up to date (~100ms tick).  Call this on the window or panel you are
about to work with. It ensures new widgets appearing in that subtree
are assigned refs automatically.  Unobserve when you are done.
A shallow root observer (depth 0) already covers top-level windows.

ref.description: Root widget ref to observe. Empty = all visible windows.

maxDepth.description: Maximum depth to walk from the root.

tools.name: SlateInspectorToolset.SlateInspectorToolset.ListObservers
tools.description:
List all active observers as a JSON array for debugging.
Each entry includes the observer identifier, whether it is the root observer,
the root widget ref (if any), max depth, and cached snapshot size.

tools.name: SlateInspectorToolset.SlateInspectorToolset.Hover
tools.description: Hover over a Slate widget, triggering any hover state or tooltip.

tools.name: SlateInspectorToolset.SlateInspectorToolset.FillForm
tools.description: Fill multiple Slate form fields at once.

fields.description: Array of {Ref, Value, FieldType} where FieldType is "textbox", "checkbox", or "combobox".

If no inspection data exists, returns an error prompting re-execution.

same graph, you MUST call this tool (and ExecuteGraphInstance) on only one actor at a time.
Wait for each call to fully complete before calling on the next actor. Concurrent calls on
actors sharing the same graph will cause a freeze.

toolsets.name: SemanticSearchToolset.SemanticSearchToolset
toolsets.description:
Toolset exposing the SemanticSearch plugin's hybrid vector + BM25 asset search
to the AI Toolset Registry.

tools.name: SemanticSearchToolset.SemanticSearchToolset.Search
tools.description: Run a semantic search over the Content Browser assets indexed by the SemanticSearch plugin.

classFilter.description: If non-empty, array of classes to filter by (provide full ref path of the class). Passing a base class covers all its subclasses. The SemanticSearch index currently covers these base classes: Blueprint, StaticMesh, SkeletalMesh, Texture, Material, MaterialInstance. Pass an empty array to consider all indexed classes.

pathRegexes.description: If non-empty, only assets whose soft object path matches at least one of these regular expressions are considered (e.g. "^/Game/Blueprints/.*"). Pass an empty array to consider all indexed paths.

k.description: Maximum number of results to return. Must be >= 1. Defaults to 10.

tools.name: SemanticSearchToolset.SemanticSearchToolset.FindSimilar
tools.description:
Find assets whose embeddings are semantically similar to the given asset's embedding.
Vector-only (no BM25). The source asset must already be indexed by the SemanticSearch
plugin.

classFilter.description: Same semantics as Search::ClassFilter. See Search for the list of currently-indexed base classes. Pass an empty array for no class filter.

pathRegexes.description: Same semantics as Search::PathRegexes. Pass an empty array for no path filter.

k.description: Maximum number of results to return. Must be >= 1. Defaults to 10.

The condition must be passed as an FInstancedStruct containing an FWorldConditionBase-derived struct.

condition.description: The instanced struct holding the world condition.

toolsets.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_Info
toolsets.description:
Niagara Toolset for general Niagara information and guidance.

Provides:
- Enum value lookups
- General usage information

Call these functions when you need context about Niagara types.

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_Info.UEnum_Info
tools.description:
Returns information about a UEnum and all its values.
ALWAYS call this when working with a UEnum type to see valid values.

enum.description: Represents a reference to a UObject or UClass.

toolsets.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_Component
toolsets.description:
Niagara Toolset for Niagara Component operations.

Use this toolset when working with:
- Niagara Components on actors
- Runtime Niagara system manipulation
- User variable overrides on component instances
- FX-related operations in levels or blueprints

This is the primary toolset for runtime Niagara operations.

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_Component.SetVariable
tools.description:
Sets the value of a user variable on the component.
This overrides the default value of a user-exposed parameter on a specific component instance.

component.description: The Niagara component to set the variable on

variable.description: The variable instance containing the name and new value to set

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_Component.SetSystem
tools.description:
Sets the Niagara System for a component.
Use this instead of setting the Asset property directly to ensure proper initialization.

niagaraComponent.description: The Niagara component to set the system on

system.description: The Niagara system asset to assign to the component

bResetExistingOverrideParameters.description: If true, reset all user variables to system defaults; if false, preserve matching overrides

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_Component.GetVariable
tools.description:
Gets the current value of a specific user variable on the component.
This retrieves the current value of a user-exposed parameter, including any component-level overrides.

component.description: The Niagara component to retrieve the variable from

var.description: The variable definition (name and type) to look up

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_Component.GetUserVariables
tools.description:
Returns all user variable values currently set on the component.
This retrieves the current values of all user-exposed parameters that can be overridden at the component level.

component.description: The Niagara component to retrieve user variables from

toolsets.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_Blueprint
toolsets.description:
Niagara Toolset for Niagara and Blueprint integration.

Provides utilities for creating Blueprint wrappers around Niagara Systems,
making it easier to expose Niagara effects as reusable Blueprint actors.

Use this toolset when you need to create Blueprint-based FX actors from Niagara systems.

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_Blueprint.ConstructNiagaraBPWrapperFromSystem
tools.description:
Creates a Blueprint actor wrapper around a Niagara System.
This generates a new Blueprint actor with a Niagara component configured to use the specified system.

Naming convention: NS_MyEffect -> B_MyEffect

newAssetPath.description: Full path for the new Blueprint asset (prefer same directory as System)

system.description: The Niagara System to wrap in the Blueprint

parentClass.description: The parent Actor class for the Blueprint (e.g., AActor)

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_Blueprint.ConstructNiagaraBPWrapperFromComponent
tools.description:
Creates a Blueprint actor wrapper from a Niagara Component.
This generates a new Blueprint actor and preserves all component property values and user variable overrides.

Naming convention: NS_MyEffect -> B_MyEffect

newAssetPath.description: Full path for the new Blueprint asset (prefer same directory as System)

component.description: The Niagara Component with configured properties and overrides to preserve

parentClass.description: The parent Actor class for the Blueprint (e.g., AActor)

toolsets.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_System
toolsets.description:
Niagara Toolset for Niagara System operations.

Provides comprehensive access to Niagara System editing, including:
- System, emitter, and module creation and modification
- Schema discovery for understanding structure
- Topology and Summary inspection for viewing current configuration
- Data access for reading and writing properties
- Dependency rollups via GetSystemDependencies
- Dynamic-input chain traversal via GetDynamicInputChain

This is the primary toolset for working with Niagara Systems in the editor.

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_System.SetSystemData
tools.description:
Sets property values on a Niagara System.
Applies new values to system-level properties based on the provided data structure.

systemData.description: Data structure containing the property values to set

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_System.SetStackInputData
tools.description:
Sets the value of a stack module input and returns the resulting stored value.
Updates the value and configuration for a specific module input parameter.

stackInputRef.description: Reference to the stack input to modify

inputData.description: The new value and configuration to apply to the input

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_System.SetRendererData
tools.description:
Sets property values on a Niagara Renderer.
Applies new values to renderer properties based on the provided data structure.
Payload shape varies with the concrete renderer class; call GetRendererSchema for the
renderer's class to inspect valid properties before writing.

renderer.description: Reference to the renderer to modify

rendererData.description: Data structure containing the property values to set

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_System.SetModuleEnabled
tools.description:
Sets whether a module is enabled.
Disabled modules remain in the stack but don't execute. Current state is visible in module topology.

moduleRef.description: Reference to the module to enable or disable

bEnabled.description: True to enable the module, false to disable it

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_System.SetEmitterData
tools.description:
Sets property values on a Niagara Emitter.
Applies new values to emitter-level properties based on the provided data structure.

emitter.description: Reference to the emitter to modify

emitterData.description: Data structure containing the property values to set

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_System.RemoveUserVariables
tools.description:
Removes user variables from a system.
Deletes the specified user variables from the system's user parameter collection.

system.description: The Niagara System to remove variables from

variablesToRemove.description: Array of variable definitions identifying which variables to remove

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_System.RemoveSetParameterEntry
tools.description:
Removes a parameter from an existing SetParameters module by name.
The module referenced by ModuleRef must be a SetParameters (UNiagaraNodeAssignment) module.
Use bIsSetParametersModule in the module topology to confirm before calling.

moduleRef.description: Reference to the existing SetParameters module

parameterName.description: Name of the parameter to remove

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_System.RemoveRenderer
tools.description:
Removes a renderer from an emitter.
Deletes the specified renderer from the emitter's renderer list.

rendererToRemove.description: Reference to the renderer to remove

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_System.RemoveModule
tools.description:
Removes a module from a script stack.
Deletes the specified module and all its inputs from the script's execution stack.

moduleToRemove.description: Reference to the module to remove from the stack

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_System.RemoveEmitter
tools.description:
Removes an emitter from a system.
Deletes the specified emitter and all its associated scripts, modules, and renderers.

emitterToRemove.description: Reference to the emitter to remove from the system

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_System.GetUserVariables
tools.description:
Returns all user variables defined on the system.
User variables are parameters exposed for external control and can be overridden per component instance.

system.description: The Niagara System to retrieve user variables from

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_System.GetSystemSummary
tools.description:
Returns lightweight system metadata: name, user variables, and one summary entry per emitter.
Use this for first contact with an unfamiliar system. For full structural detail call GetEmitterTopology per emitter.

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_System.GetSystemSchema
tools.description:
Returns property schema for Niagara System.
Describes all available properties and their types that can be set on a Niagara System.

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_System.GetSystemDependencies
tools.description:
Returns the four Used* sets (renderers, data interfaces, modules, dynamic inputs)
gathered across all emitters and system scripts.
These sets are not included in topology structs; call this endpoint separately when needed.

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_System.GetSystemData
tools.description:
Returns system property values.
Retrieves the current values of all configurable system-level properties.

system.description: The Niagara System to retrieve data from

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_System.GetSystemCompileState
tools.description:
Returns the current compile state of a Niagara System: aggregate status, per-script compile
events, and summary flags. Waits for any in-flight compile to complete before collecting.

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_System.GetStackIssues
tools.description:
Returns all stack issues (errors, warnings, info) from the Niagara module stack, including
dismissed ones. Waits for any in-flight compile to complete before collecting.

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_System.GetStackInputTopology
tools.description:
Returns stack input topology: name, type, visibility, editability. No value payload.
For the resolved value call GetStackInputData. For a dynamic-input chain call GetDynamicInputChain.

stackInputRef.description: Reference to the stack input to describe.

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_System.GetStackInputSchema
tools.description:
Returns schema for a single module input in the stack.
Describes the type, metadata, and configuration options for a specific input parameter.

inputReference.description: Reference to the stack input to get the schema for

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_System.GetStackInputData
tools.description:
Returns the value of a stack module input.
Retrieves the current value and configuration for a specific module input parameter.

stackInputRef.description: Reference to the stack input to retrieve data from

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_System.GetScriptStackTopology
tools.description:
Returns script stack topology: all modules and their inputs in execution order.
All fields always populated.

scriptRef.description: Reference to the script stack to describe.

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_System.GetScriptStackInputValues
tools.description:
Returns all resolved input values for every module in the given script stack.
One FNiagaraExt_ModuleInputValues entry per module.

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_System.GetRendererSchema
tools.description:
Returns property schema for a specific Renderer class.
Describes all available properties and their types for the given renderer type.

rendererClass.description: The renderer class to get the schema for (e.g., UNiagaraSpriteRendererProperties)

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_System.GetRendererData
tools.description:
Returns renderer property values.
Retrieves the current values of all configurable renderer properties.

rendererRef.description: Reference to the renderer to retrieve data from

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_System.GetModuleTopology
tools.description:
Returns module topology: metadata and all inputs (name/type/visibility only, no values).
All fields always populated.

moduleRef.description: Reference to the module to describe.

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_System.GetModuleSchemaFromAsset
tools.description:
Returns schema for a module asset.
Standalone function that doesn't require a system context - useful for browsing available modules.

moduleAsset.description: The module script asset to get the schema for

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_System.GetModuleSchema
tools.description:
Returns schema for a module and all its inputs.
Call this after seeing a module in topology to understand what inputs it exposes.

moduleReference.description: Reference to the module to get the schema for

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_System.GetModuleInputValues
tools.description:
Returns resolved input values for a single module.
Use when you need values for one specific module without walking the whole emitter.

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_System.GetEmitterTopology
tools.description:
Returns full emitter topology: four script stacks with all modules and inputs, renderer references.
All fields always populated. The returned topology carries no input values; call GetEmitterInputValues in parallel.
Note: mutation endpoints (AddEmitter, AddModule) also return topology structs — input values require a separate data call.

emitterRef.description: Reference to the emitter to describe.

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_System.GetEmitterSummary
tools.description:
Returns lightweight emitter metadata: name, enabled state, sim target, renderer classes.
Use this when you only need to check metadata without walking the full emitter structure.

emitterRef.description: Reference to the emitter to summarise.

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_System.GetEmitterSchema
tools.description:
Returns property schema for Niagara Emitter.
Describes all available properties and their types that can be set on a Niagara Emitter.

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_System.GetEmitterInputValues
tools.description:
Returns all resolved input values for every module across all four emitter script stacks.
One FNiagaraExt_ModuleInputValues entry per module, each carrying all its resolved input values.
Call this in parallel with GetEmitterTopology to get both structure and values in two passes.

emitterRef.description: Reference to the emitter to retrieve input values from

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_System.GetEmitterData
tools.description:
Returns emitter property values as a single JSON-string blob in PropertyValues.
The blob contains the full FVersionedNiagaraEmitterData (SimTarget, bLocalSpace, RandomSeed,
FixedBounds, etc.) — fields use C++ PascalCase, must be parsed to read individual values.

For typed access to common metadata (SimTarget, EmitterName, bEnabled, RendererClasses) prefer
GetEmitterSummary — it returns those as named fields directly and avoids a JSON parse step.
Use this endpoint when you need the full property set or a less-common field.

emitterRef.description: Reference to the emitter to retrieve data from

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_System.GetDynamicInputSchemaFromAsset
tools.description:
Returns schema for a dynamic input asset.
Standalone function that doesn't require a system context - useful for browsing available dynamic inputs.

dynamicInputAsset.description: The dynamic input script asset to get the schema for

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_System.GetDynamicInputSchema
tools.description:
Returns schema for a dynamic input module in the stack.
Describes the inputs and configuration for a procedural value generator.

dynamicInputReference.description: Reference to the dynamic input to get the schema for

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_System.GetDynamicInputChain
tools.description:
Returns the full recursive chain for a dynamic input: topology metadata and resolved values at every level.
The starting input must have value mode Dynamic; an error is surfaced otherwise.
The schema expands one full level and emits a typed recursion stub at deeper levels;
the wire format recurses to arbitrary depth matching the underlying chain.

stackInputRef.description: Reference to the dynamic input to traverse

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_System.GetDataInterfaceSchema
tools.description:
Returns property schema for a specific Data Interface class.
Describes all available properties and their types for the given data interface type.

dataInterfaceClass.description: The data interface class to get the schema for

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_System.GetAvailableDynamicInputs
tools.description:
Returns all available Dynamic Input Module assets compatible with the given type.
Dynamic inputs provide procedural value generation for module parameters.

type.description: The Niagara type definition to find compatible dynamic inputs for

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_System.CreateNiagaraSystem
tools.description:
Creates a new Niagara System asset.
The new system will be based on the template system, inheriting its configuration and emitters.

assetName.description: Name of the new asset (without path or extension)

assetPath.description: Directory path where the new asset will be created

templateSystem.description: Template system to base the new system on (required)

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_System.ApplyStackIssueFix
tools.description:
Applies a Fix-style stack issue fix identified by IssueId and FixId. Link-style fixes are
rejected. The fix is undoable via the editor undo stack. Applying a fix may trigger a
recompile; the result waits for that compile to complete so post-fix state is valid.

system.description: The Niagara System to apply the fix to.

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_System.AddUserVariables
tools.description:
Adds or updates user variables on a system.
If a variable with the same name already exists, it will be replaced with the new definition.

system.description: The Niagara System to add variables to

variablesToAdd.description: Array of user variables to add or update

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_System.AddSetParametersModule
tools.description:
Adds a SetParameters module to a script stack.
Unlike AddModule which requires a script asset, a SetParameters module dynamically assigns
values to named parameters and generates its own internal script. Use this when you need
to set one or more particle/emitter/system parameters directly in the stack.

moduleLocationRef.description: Reference specifying where to add the module

parameters.description: Array of parameter entries, each with a variable (name + type) and an optional default value string

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_System.AddSetParameterEntry
tools.description:
Adds a single parameter to an existing SetParameters module.
The module referenced by ModuleRef must be a SetParameters (UNiagaraNodeAssignment) module.
Use bIsSetParametersModule in the module topology to confirm before calling.

moduleRef.description: Reference to the existing SetParameters module

entry.description: The parameter to add (variable name, type, and optional default value string)

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_System.AddRenderer
tools.description:
Adds a renderer to an emitter.
Creates a new renderer of the specified type and adds it to the emitter's renderer list.
Returns an FNiagaraExt_RendererRef with the new renderer's Index and RendererClass,
usable directly with SetRendererData / GetRendererData without a follow-up topology call.

newRendererLocation.description: Reference specifying which emitter to add the renderer to

rendererClass.description: The class of renderer to create (e.g., UNiagaraSpriteRendererProperties)

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_System.AddModule
tools.description:
Adds a module to a script stack.
The module will be inserted into the specified script's execution stack.
Returns the module topology with all inputs walked (no input values — call GetModuleInputValues for values).

moduleLocationRef.description: Reference specifying where to add the module

moduleAsset.description: The module script asset to add to the stack

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_System.AddEmitter
tools.description:
Adds an emitter to a Niagara System.
The new emitter will be based on the template emitter, inheriting its configuration and modules.
Returns the full emitter topology (no input values — call GetEmitterInputValues for values).

system.description: The Niagara System to add the emitter to

templateEmitter.description: The emitter asset to use as a template for the new emitter

emitterName.description: Name for the new emitter instance

toolsets.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_Assets
toolsets.description:
Niagara Toolset for discovery of Niagara script assets.

Provides:
- GetAssetDiscoveryInfo: project-configured asset discovery groups
- FindNiagaraScripts: asset registry search filtered by usage, visibility, name, and module usage bitmask
- GetNiagaraScriptDigest: decoded asset-registry tags for a single script

All functions read from the asset registry without LoadObject. Pair with
NiagaraToolset_System.AddModule to drop discovered modules into a stack.

Note: FAssetData JSON serialization does not include the TagsAndValues map (it
is not a UPROPERTY), so callers wanting decoded metadata for a result row from
FindNiagaraScripts must round-trip through GetNiagaraScriptDigest.

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_Assets.GetNiagaraScriptDigest
tools.description:
Returns the decoded asset-registry tag metadata for a Niagara script asset.

Looks up the asset by object path in the asset registry and reads its tags;
no LoadObject is performed. Returned fields reflect the exposed (published)
version when the script uses FVersionedNiagaraScriptData versioning - the
registry never carries non-exposed-version metadata.

objectPath.description: Full object path of the script (e.g. "/Niagara/Modules/Spawn/Initialize Particle.Initialize Particle").

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_Assets.GetAssetDiscoveryInfo
tools.description:
Returns the project's configured asset discovery groups.
Each group describes a content directory's purpose and paths.

tools.name: NiagaraToolsets.NiagaraToolset_Assets.FindNiagaraScripts
tools.description:
Searches for UNiagaraScript assets matching the given filters.

Reads filterable metadata from asset registry tags only - no LoadObject required.

Tags reflect the exposed (published) version of versioned scripts; this
function does not need a version filter and there is no way to discover
non-exposed versions through the asset registry.

folderPath.description: The folder to search within. Pass an empty string to search the entire project.

name.description: If non-empty, only return assets whose name contains this substring (case-insensitive).

usages.description: If non-empty, only return assets whose Usage matches one of the listed values. Empty array = all usages allowed.

visibilities.description: If non-empty, only return assets whose LibraryVisibility matches one of the listed values. Empty array defaults to {Library} (the editor's "Exposed") - explicit override required to surface Unexposed or Hidden scripts.

moduleUsageBitmask.description: If non-zero, restricts results to Module scripts whose ModuleUsageBitmask shares at least one bit with this argument (any-match). Non-Module scripts are excluded when this is non-zero. Pass 0 to disable the bitmask gate.

bRecursive.description: Whether to search subfolders. Ignored when FolderPath is empty (whole-project scan is always exhaustive).

bIncludeDeprecated.description: If false (default usage), assets whose exposed version has bDeprecated=true are excluded. Pass true only when the caller explicitly wants to surface deprecated assets (e.g. searching for the canonical replacement of a known deprecated module).

toolsets.name: UMGToolSet.UMGToolSet
toolsets.description:
UMG widget toolset for AI-driven widget creation and tree manipulation.

IMPORTANT WORKFLOW - for every widget and slot returned by this toolset:
  1. Call ObjectTools.list_properties(widget) to discover exact property names.
  2. Call ObjectTools.get_properties(widget, [...]) with those exact names.
  3. Call ObjectTools.set_properties(widget, {...}) with those exact names.
Property names vary per widget class and CANNOT be guessed - list_properties is required.
Skipping step 1 causes set_properties to silently fail or set wrong properties.

Returns UObject pointers - ToolsetRegistry serializes them as {"refPath": "..."} automatically.
Pass returned Widget, Slot, and Parent pointers directly to ObjectTools or back to this toolset.

tools.name: UMGToolSet.UMGToolSet.WrapWidgets
tools.description:
Wraps one or more widgets in a new panel widget of the specified class.
Only the root-most widgets in the selection are wrapped — children of other selected widgets
are skipped because their parent will be wrapped. Returns info for each newly created wrapper.

Use ObjectTools.list_properties on each returned Widget and Slot to discover property names
before calling set_properties (padding, alignment, anchors, etc. vary per panel class).

widgets.description: The widgets to wrap. Must all be in WidgetBlueprint's tree.

wrapperClass.description: The panel widget class to wrap with (must be a UPanelWidget subclass).

tools.name: UMGToolSet.UMGToolSet.ToggleWidgetAsVariable
tools.description: Sets the bIsVariable flag.

bIsVariable.description: True to expose as a blueprint variable, false to hide it.

tools.name: UMGToolSet.UMGToolSet.SetNamedSlotContent
tools.description: Sets content for a named slot. Returns full widget info including Slot pointer.

hostWidget.description: The widget that owns the named slot, or null to target the root WidgetTree.

slotName.description: Name of the slot to fill (e.g., "content", "header").

widgetClass.description: The widget class to place in the slot.

widgetName.description: Name for the new widget instance.

tools.name: UMGToolSet.UMGToolSet.ReplaceWidgetWithTemplate
tools.description:
Replaces a widget instance in the blueprint's widget tree with a new instance created from a
different template widget class. Preserves references for members that exist on both classes
with a compatible type/signature: bindings, BP graph variable references, animation
bindings, and delegate bindings. Members without a compatible counterpart on the new class
are listed in the returned report; references to those members in the outer blueprint will
become orphaned graph nodes / dangling bindings.

widgetBlueprint.description: The widget blueprint containing the widget to replace.

widgetToReplace.description: The widget instance to replace (must be in WidgetBlueprint's tree).

templateClass.description: The widget class to create the replacement from.

tools.name: UMGToolSet.UMGToolSet.ReplaceWidgetWithNamedSlot
tools.description:
Replaces a host widget with the content of one of its named slots. The host must implement
INamedSlotInterface (e.g., a UUserWidget exposing named slots). The slot's content widget is
moved up to take the host's place in the tree.

widgetBlueprint.description: The widget blueprint containing the host widget.

namedSlot.description: The slot whose content replaces WidgetToReplace.

tools.name: UMGToolSet.UMGToolSet.ReplaceWidgetWithChild
tools.description:
Replaces a panel widget with its first child, removing the panel from the tree.
The widget to replace must be a UPanelWidget with only one child.

widgetBlueprint.description: The widget blueprint containing the panel widget.

widgetToReplace.description: The panel widget to replace with its first child.

tools.name: UMGToolSet.UMGToolSet.RenameWidget
tools.description: Renames a widget. Returns updated widget info or empty on failure.

tools.name: UMGToolSet.UMGToolSet.RemoveWidget
tools.description: Removes a widget and its children from the tree.

widget.description: The widget to remove (along with its children).

tools.name: UMGToolSet.UMGToolSet.RemoveUIComponent
tools.description: Removes a UI component of the given class from the named widget.

widgetName.description: Name of the widget instance to remove the component from.

tools.name: UMGToolSet.UMGToolSet.MoveWidget
tools.description: Moves a widget to a new parent panel at the specified position. Returns updated widget info with new Slot.

childIndex.description: Position in new parent's child list (0 = first child). -1 (default) appends to end.

tools.name: UMGToolSet.UMGToolSet.MoveUIComponent
tools.description: Moves a UI component before or after another component on the same widget.

widgetName.description: Name of the widget instance whose component to move.

relativeToComponentClass.description: The UIComponent subclass to move relative to.

bMoveAfter.description: True to place after RelativeToComponentClass, false to place before.

tools.name: UMGToolSet.UMGToolSet.ListWidgetClasses
tools.description: Lists available widget classes, optionally filtered by name substring.

filter.description: Substring to match against class names. Pass empty string to return all classes.

tools.name: UMGToolSet.UMGToolSet.ListWidgetBlueprints
tools.description: Lists widget blueprints in a content folder.

folderPath.description: Content folder to search, e.g. "/Game/UI". Searches recursively.

tools.name: UMGToolSet.UMGToolSet.GetWidgetTreeDepth
tools.description: Returns the maximum depth of the widget tree. Depth: root with no children = 0; root + children = 1; etc.

widgetBlueprint.description: Represents a reference to a UObject or UClass.

startWidget.description: Represents a reference to a UObject or UClass.

tools.name: UMGToolSet.UMGToolSet.GetWidgets
tools.description:
Returns blueprint info and all widgets in depth-first order.
Children within each parent are in their panel slot order - this is the hierarchy order shown in the designer.
Info contains ParentClass (pass to CreateWidgetBlueprint) and RootWidgetClass.
Use ObjectTools.list_properties on each returned Widget and Slot to get property names before calling set_properties.

widgetBlueprint.description: The widget blueprint asset (e.g. "/Game/UI/WBP_MyWidget"), excluding the "_C" suffix.

tools.name: UMGToolSet.UMGToolSet.GetWidgetDescription
tools.description:
Full property dump of every widget in the tree.
Each line: [N] Type Name  Prop:Value ...  slot:(SlotProp:Value ...)
N is the 0-based index into result.Widgets -- use result.Widgets[N] to get the widget ref without text parsing.

Same indentation format as GetTaggedWidgetDescription; richer per-widget detail.

maxDepth.description: 1 = no limit; 0 = StartWidget only; N = N levels.

tools.name: UMGToolSet.UMGToolSet.GetWidgetClassInfo
tools.description:
Returns the Category, Description and if it's a Panel for a single widget class.
Same per-entry data as ListWidgetClasses, but lets callers query a class they already have
without scanning every UClass. Returns an empty entry if WidgetClass is null.
Can be used to get more information on the class from the Description and Category.

tools.name: UMGToolSet.UMGToolSet.GetNamedSlots
tools.description: Returns named slot bindings (separate from tree hierarchy).

tools.name: UMGToolSet.UMGToolSet.CreateWidgetBlueprint
tools.description: Creates a new Widget Blueprint asset. Returns the blueprint or nullptr on failure.

assetName.description: Name for the new blueprint asset.

parentClass.description: The parent UUserWidget class. Get this from GetWidgets Info.ParentClass on the source blueprint.

tools.name: UMGToolSet.UMGToolSet.CompileWidgetBlueprint
tools.description:
Compiles a widget blueprint. Returns false with error details if compilation fails.
Errors include missing BindWidget bindings, type mismatches, and graph errors.
Call after all widgets and properties are set. Save separately via AssetTools.save_asset.

tools.name: UMGToolSet.UMGToolSet.BindToEventProperty
tools.description:
Adds a Blueprint event handler graph node bound to a widget's multicast delegate event,

Typical events: UButton::OnClicked / OnPressed / OnReleased / OnHovered / OnUnhovered,
UCheckBox::OnCheckStateChanged, USlider::OnValueChanged. The matching delegate
UPROPERTY must exist on PropertyClass (or a parent of it).

Preconditions:
  - PropertyName must exist in the blueprint.
  - PropertyClass must be the widget's class (or a parent class) that declares the delegate.

widgetBlueprint.description: The widget blueprint that will own the event handler.

eventName.description: Name of the multicast delegate UPROPERTY on PropertyClass (e.g. "OnClicked").

propertyName.description: Name of the blueprint variable owning the event.

propertyClass.description: Class declaring the delegate, typically the widget's class (e.g. UButton::StaticClass()).

tools.name: UMGToolSet.UMGToolSet.AddWidget
tools.description:
Adds a widget to the tree at the specified position. Returns full widget info including Slot pointer.
When ParentWidget is null and no root exists, the new widget becomes the root of the tree.
Use ObjectTools.list_properties on the returned Widget and Slot to get property names before calling set_properties.

widgetDisplayName.description: Display name for the new widget instance.

parentWidget.description: The panel widget to add to. Pass null to add to root, or to make this widget the root if the tree is empty.

childIndex.description: Position in parent's child list (0 = first child). -1 (default) appends to end.

tools.name: UMGToolSet.UMGToolSet.AddUIComponent
tools.description: Adds a UI component of the given class to the named widget.

widgetName.description: Name of the widget instance to add the component to.
